behavior_summary: This script implements the behavior and dialogue for a guardsman NPC. It covers: examine/U8P_GUARDMAN::look responses, area-triggered ambient/patrol chatter, a short 'U8P_GUARDMAN::cast' alarm voice, a player conversation with a small menu, and cleanup when an actor leaves the guardsman's trigger area.
U8P_GUARDMAN::look: a simple examine handler — if the NPC is dead it barks a "dead guardsman" line, otherwise a standard "guardsman" line and the interaction ends.

U8P_GUARDMAN::enterFastArea: triggered when an actor enters the guard's fast/trigger area; performs guard-alive and map checks, clears several reference slots (resetRef) and (when allowed) calls U8P_GUARDMAN::func095B to produce ambient/patrol barks or start short guard activity.
U8P_GUARDMAN::leaveFastArea: clears the same set of area-linked references via resetRef to tear down any area state when the actor leaves.
U8P_GUARDMAN::cast(uword): a short monologue invoked on a U8P_GUARDMAN::cast-like event that barks an alarm line ('Guardsmen, to arms!'). The parameter is declared but not observed to affect output.

U8P_GUARDMAN::use: the conversation entry. It opens a small menu (Who are you?, What is your duty?, Bye), handles player choices (suspending to capture selection), branches dialogue text based on a global flag (devonInRule) and random selection (urandom), and contains an early 'refusal' path that sets somebodyTalking, spawns a short process, and emits a refusal bark when an item/frame check triggers (Item::getFrame == 0x14).

U8P_GUARDMAN::func095B: ambient/patrol routine invoked from area entry and elsewhere. It gates output on distance and somebodyTalking, uses urandom to choose lines, checks for nearby NPC/combat targets and can run a short combat-response sequence (calling METHOD::* helpers and emitting aggressive barks). It uses spawn/suspend patterns to space utterances.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GUARDMAN::enterFastArea has early-exit guards: Npc::isDead(this) -> skip, Npc::getMap(this) == specific map -> skip, and a 'secondexecution' flag prevents repeat execution.
U8P_GUARDMAN::use implements a menu loop: build menu (local2), Ask + SuspendAssign into local1, then strcmp-driven branching per choice; MenuRemove reduces options as they are chosen and 'Bye' exits.

U8P_GUARDMAN::use contains an interrupt/refusal path: if Item::getFrame(local3) == 0x14 then set somebodyTalking=1, spawn FREE::func1C19 (short process), suspend/bark a refusal, then clear somebodyTalking — preventing normal conversation.

U8P_GUARDMAN::func095B gate logic: checks FREE::getDistanceBetween and somebodyTalking (read) to avoid barks when player is far or another conversation is in progress; uses urandom to vary lines and spawns short processes for timing between lines.
open_questions: Exact semantics of METHOD::func091C and METHOD::func15E7 called from dialogue/behavior routines — their direct game-state effects are not present in the structured output and remain inferred (likely activity/state updates).
Precise meaning of resetRef ref IDs (0x0205, 0x0204, 0x00F0, 0x0200): they clearly clear area-linked references but what engine subsystems they map to is unspecified.
The Item::getFrame(...) == 0x14 check triggers a refusal path in U8P_GUARDMAN::use — what concrete gameplay condition or equipped item frame this represents is unknown.
Whether the uword parameter of U8P_GUARDMAN::cast affects behavior in other contexts (it is present but unused in observed nodes).
The nuance of the 'devonInRule' flag: it toggles allegiance/duty phrasing in dialogue but its world-story trigger and scope remain external to this script.

main_function.name: U8P_GUARDMAN::look
main_function.purpose: Handle player examine/U8P_GUARDMAN::look interaction: produce a short descriptive bark depending on life state (dead vs alive) and end the U8P_GUARDMAN::look interaction.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player examines/looks at the guardsman NPC.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARDMAN::enterFastArea
function_map.role: area-entry behavior; prepare/trigger ambient patrol speech

function_map.name: U8P_GUARDMAN::cast
function_map.role: monologue/emote when a spell/ability event occurs (alarm bark)

function_map.name: U8P_GUARDMAN::use
function_map.role: player conversation/dialogue entry with menu, branching and refusal path

function_map.name: U8P_GUARDMAN::func095B
function_map.role: ambient/patrol monologue routine (randomized barks, combat-response branch)

internal_calls.purpose: Trigger ambient/patrol speech and short behavior sequence upon area entry

external_calls.purpose: Decide whether to play a 'dead guardsman' or normal 'guardsman' U8P_GUARDMAN::look line
Skip area-entry behavior on specific maps
Inspect an item/frame (local1) to decide an internal conditional path
Clear area-linked references for this NPC before starting new behavior
Tear down area-linked references when an actor leaves the trigger
Capture NPC facing/direction at conversation start (stored in local4)
If checked and equals 0x14, take refusal/interrupt path (set somebodyTalking and spawn short process)
Housekeeping resetRef call during conversation startup (reset a reference slot)
Spawn a short transient process used as a timing/interrupt during refusal path
Called from dialogue flow to trigger an external side-effect (likely an activity/state change)
Compute distance to actor/player and suppress barks when actor is far
Detect whether a referenced item corresponds to an NPC for a potential combat-response branch
Check if the nearby NPC is in combat to trigger aggressive response lines
Part of the combat-response sequence; likely changes NPC state or starts a reaction
Auxiliary helper invoked around certain barks; likely handles animation/state or sound
Spawn short processes used as delays between lines/actions
Randomize greeting/duty phrasing to add variety to dialogue responses
Select which ambient/patrol line to speak
Change NPC activity/state as a side-effect after certain dialogue branches

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Selects alternate allegiance/duty lines in conversation (alters phrasing of 'Who are you?' and 'What is your duty?' responses).

reads.name: somebodyTalking
reads.likely_effect: Checked by U8P_GUARDMAN::func095B to suppress ambient barks if another conversation/speech is in progress.

reads.name: secondexecution
reads.likely_effect: Prevents U8P_GUARDMAN::enterFastArea from re-running its area-entry behavior on repeat entries.

writes.name: somebodyTalking
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the guard refuses to speak (prevents overlapping conversations), then cleared to 0 after the interrupt/refusal sequence.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the behavior and dialogue for a guardsman NPC. It covers: examine/U8P_GUARDMAN::look responses, area-triggered ambient/patrol chatter, a short 'U8P_GUARDMAN::cast' alarm voice, a player conversation with a small menu, and cleanup when an actor leaves the guardsman's trigger area.
